decision_summary.rationale: Removing provisioned concurrency for the idle Lambda function eliminates $37.84/month of waste without operational risk.

llm_response.technical_explanation: The Lambda function 'order-processor-prod' has provisioned concurrency set to 5 but has received 0 invocations in the last 30 days. This means the provisioned concurrency is not being utilized, resulting in unnecessary costs. Removing the provisioned concurrency will allow the function to scale on-demand, which is free up to 1 million requests per month.
llm_response.terraform_block:
# Remove the aws_lambda_provisioned_concurrency_config resource block for the order-processor-prod function
# This will allow the function to scale on-demand, eliminating the $37.84/month charge for idle provisioned concurrency

# aws_lambda_provisioned_concurrency_config "order_processor_prod_pc" {
#   function_name              = aws_lambda_function.order_processor_prod.function_name
#   qualifier                  = "PROVISIONED"
#   provisioned_concurrent_executions = 5
# }

resource "aws_lambda_function" "order_processor_prod" {
  filename      = "order_processor.zip"
  function_name = "order-processor-prod"
  role          = aws_iam_role.lambda_exec.arn
  handler       = "index.handler"
  runtime       = "nodejs18.x"
  memory_size   = 512
  timeout       = 30

  tags = {
    Name        = "order-processor-prod"
    Environment = "production"
    FinOpsAction = "REMOVE_PROVISIONED_CONCURRENCY"
    FinOpsReviewed = "true"
  }
}

terraform_validate.name: terraform_validate

checkov.name: checkov
checkov.details: No applicable Checkov checks for this resource type

nl_quality.name: nl_quality
nl_quality.details: The report covers core facts (provisioned_concurrency=5, 0 invocations, $37.84 waste) with correct numbers, but omits that removing the config block does not affect the function itself and that data_loss_acknowledged=false is a confirmed safe non-issue.
